headline: Is Exelon’s Bigger 2026–2029 Grid Spend and Q1 Beat Altering The Investment Case For Exelon (EXC)?
summary: In the past week, Exelon reported first-quarter 2026 results that exceeded expectations, with revenue rising year over year and adjusted net income edging higher, while reaffirming full-year earnings guidance of US$2.81–US$2.91 per share. The company also raised its multiyear capital plan to US$41.70 billion for 2026–2029, signaling a larger pipeline of grid and infrastructure investments that could influence how investors think about its long-term earnings profile and asset base. Next,...

headline: A Look At Exelon (EXC) Valuation After Recent Share Price Swings
summary: Why Exelon (EXC) is on investors’ radar Exelon (EXC) has drawn fresh attention after recent share price moves, with the stock up around 4% over the past month but down about 7% over the past 3 months. For a regulated utility focused on electricity and natural gas distribution, those short term swings give you a reason to pause and reassess how its current valuation lines up with its latest fundamentals. headline: How Recent Analyst Shifts Are Rewriting the Story for Exelon (EXC)
summary: Exelon’s fair value estimate has been trimmed slightly to US$49.33 from US$49.56, keeping the headline price target change relatively small. That modest adjustment sits against mixed analyst commentary, where more cautious views around regulation and recent target cuts contrast with constructive stances from firms like JPMorgan. Some analysts see room for upside if the company sticks to its plan. Read on to see how these shifting targets fit into the broader narrative and what to watch as the...
